content.full_text:
April 17, 1975


Mr. Harry M. Free, County Clerk
County of Imperial
Courthouse
El Centro, California 92243

Dear Mr. Free:

                                Re: Request for Opinion
                                    No. 75-97

     Thank you for your letter of April 3, 1975 re-
questing an opinion with respect to your duties under
the Political Reform Act.

     Since your request raises no substantial question
involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act,
your request for a formal opinion is denied.

     Assuming that the statement was mailed by first
class registered mail, the date of the postmark is
deemed the date of filing. This is so even if the date
of the postmark is after the deadline. For example, if
a statement sent by first class registered mail is post-
marked three days after the deadline and received five
days after the deadline, a penalty may be imposed of
three days' lateness, not five days' lateness.

     If you have any further questions in this are or if
you wish further comment on the questions in your letter,
I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

                                Sincerely,


                                Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                Chairman

DHL:cjb

75 097
REQ 4/8/75

HARRY M. FREE
COUNTY CLERK

EL CENTRO, CALIFORNIA

April 3rd, 1975

Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street Suit 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sir:

We would appreciate early interpretation of
Sections 81007 and 91013, Government Code, Proposition
9. We have interpreted Government Code Section 81007
for the purpose of the postmark, registered mail, that
the postmark was applicable ONLY for the purpose of meeting
the deadline date and thereafter the postmark date was
not applicable.

We refer you to an example as candidates campaign
statements due Jan. 20th, 1975, with January 23rd being
the deadline for filing and if postmarked January 23rd,
we accepted that date as the filing date and as timely
filing and did not asses a penalty.

However, those postmarked after Jan. 23rd. were
not considered for the postmark date but determined
filed when we received them.

Example:

1. Statement postmarked registered mail Jan. 23rd,
1975, received Jan. 25, - no penalty.

2. Statement postmarked Jan. 26th, received Jan. 28th,
we assessed the penalty from Jan. 23rd to the date
received and filed, Jan. 28th.

We have discussed this with County Counsel and
have concurred that we would like your opinion at
an early date.

Very truly yours,

HARRY M. FREE, COUNTY CLERK

By: [illegible] Deputy
